hadiths.chapter: 41. CHAPTER ON THE EXTENT OF ILLNESS THAT PERMITS
hadiths.ar:
1944  -  وَّ رَّوَّى سُّلَّي ْمَّان ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّمْزٍو عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال لل َّهُّ عَّن ْهَّا عَّي ْن َّهَّا ق ِى   
س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان َّ ق َّا َّمَّزَّهَّا رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ إ َّن ْ ي ُّق ْطِزَّ وَّ ق َّ ال
hadiths.en:
Bakr ibn Muhammad al-Azdi narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}:  
"My father asked Imam {a.s} while I was listening about the extent of illness that permits 
a person to leave fasting.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'When they are unable to take suhoor (the pre-dawn meal).'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 325 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 
10, p. 219 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 41, H. 1943
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE EXTENT OF ILLNESS THAT PERMITS
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE EXTENT OF ILLNESS THAT PERMITS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤١، ح ١٩٤٣

hadiths.chapter: 41. CHAPTER ON THE EXTENT OF ILLNESS THAT PERMITS
hadiths.ar: 1945  - وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ جَّزِي ز ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  إَّلز َّمَّذِ إ َّق ْطَّزَّ.
hadiths.en:
Sulayman ibn Amr narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"Umm Salama (may Allah {SWT} be pleased with her) complained of an issue with her 
eye during the Month of Ramadan . The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him 
and his family) instructed her to break her fast and said:  'The nourishment of the night 
is restorative for your eyes.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 119 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 382 • Al-
Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 301 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 218 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, p. 322 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 41, H. 1944
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE EXTENT OF ILLNESS THAT PERMITS
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE EXTENT OF ILLNESS THAT PERMITS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤١، ح ١٩٤٤

hadiths.chapter: 41. CHAPTER ON THE EXTENT OF ILLNESS THAT PERMITS
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Hariz from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, he said:  
"A fasting person, if they fear harm to their eyes due to an eye infection, may break 
their fast."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 118 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 301 • Tafsir 
al-Safi, Part 1, p. 219 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 218 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 165 • Tafsir 
Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 2, p. 242 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 41, H. 1945
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE EXTENT OF ILLNESS THAT PERMITS
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE EXTENT OF ILLNESS THAT PERMITS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤١، ح ١٩٤٥

hadiths.chapter: 41. CHAPTER ON THE EXTENT OF ILLNESS THAT PERMITS
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "Anything that harms a person due to fasting makes breaking 
the fast obligatory for them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 301

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 41, H. 1946

hadiths.chapter: 42. WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE
hadiths.ar:
1948  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّم َّارُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّوسَّى عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّصِي ن ُّهُّ إَّلْعَّطَّس ُّ جَّي  َّى ي ح َّاف َّ عَّلَّى  
ن َّق ْسِهِ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Al-'Ala narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said:   
"I heard Abu Ja'far {a.s} say:  
'An elderly person and one suffering from chronic thirst are not at fault if they break 
their fast during the Month of Ramadan.  
Each of them should give a mudd (a specific measure) of food as charity for each day, 
and there is no obligation to make up the fast.  
If they cannot afford this, then there is nothing upon them.'"   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tafsir al-Ayyashi, vol. 1, p. 79 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 116 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 238 • Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 104 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 145 • Al-Wafi, 
Vol. 11, p. 295 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 209 • Tafsir al-Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 389 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, 
p. 320 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 42, H. 1947
reference.chapter: WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE
reference.location: Volume 2 › WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ١٩٤٧

hadiths.chapter: 42. WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE
hadiths.en:
Ammar ibn Musa narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s} regarding a man who 
becomes so thirsty that he fears for his life.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He may drink just enough to sustain himself and prevent collapse, but 
he should not drink until he is fully satisfied."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 117 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 240 
• Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 145 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 296 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 214

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 42, H. 1948
reference.chapter: WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE
reference.location: Volume 2 › WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ١٩٤٨

hadiths.chapter: 42. WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE UNABLE TO FAST; AN ELDERLY PERSON, A YOUNG PERSON,
hadiths.ar:
1950  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْعَّلاَّءُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْي ُّهُّ ي َّق ُّول مِلُّ إَّلْمُّق ْزِب ُّ وَّ 
إَّلْمُّزْص ِغُّ إَّلْق َّلِي لَّه ُّ إَّلل َّن َّن ِ لأَّ جَّزَّح َّ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إ َّن ْ ي ُّق ْطِزَّإ ق ِى  س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان َّ لِأ َّن  َّهُّمَّ ا لأَّ ي ُّطِي ق َّان ِ إَّلص َّوْمَّ وَّ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إ َّن ْ ي َّي َّصَّذ َّق َّ كُّل ُّ  
وَّإجِذَّة ٍ مِن ْهُّمَّا ق ِى  كُّلِّ ي َّوْمٍ ي ُّق ْطِزُّ ق ِي هِ ن ِمُّذٍّ مِن ْ طَّعَّامٍ وَّ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا ق َّص َّاءُّ كُّلِّ ي َّوْمٍ إ َّق ْطَّزَّإ ق ِي هِ ي ُّم َّ ي َّق ْص ِي َّان ِهِ ن َّعْذُّ.
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Ibn Bukayr, it is reported that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad 
Al-Sadiq {a.s} was asked about the verse of Allah {SWT}, the Exalted:  
"And upon those who can fast with difficulty, there is a ransom: the feeding of a poor 
person" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:184).  
Imam {a.s} said: "[This applies] to those who were able to fast but then became elderly, 
suffered from chronic thirst, or similar conditions. They must give a mudd (a specific 
measure) of food for each day."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 116 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 296 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 211 • Tafsir al-Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 387 • Tafsir al-Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 388 • Tafsir Nur Al-
Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 166 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 2, p. 242 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 42, H. 1949
reference.chapter: WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE UNABLE TO FAST; AN ELDERLY PERSON, A YOUNG PERSON,
reference.location: Volume 2 › WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE UNABLE TO FAST; AN ELDERLY PERSON, A YOUNG PERSON,

hadiths.chapter: 42. WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE UNABLE TO FAST; AN ELDERLY PERSON, A YOUNG PERSON,
hadiths.ar:
1951  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلْمَّلِك ِ ن ْن ُّ عُّي ْن َّه َّ إَّلْهَّاس ِمِى  ُّ إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلس  َّي ْح ِ إَّلْكَّن ِي زِ وَّ إَّلْعَّح ُّور ِ إَّلْكَّن ِي زَّة ِ إَّل َّي ِى  
ي َّص ْعُّف ُّ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّوْمِ ق ِى  س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان َّ ق َّال  مِن ْ جِن ْطَّه ٍ
hadiths.en:
Al-'Ala narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far {a.s}, who said:  
"I heard Imam {a.s} say:  
'A pregnant woman in her late term and a nursing woman with little milk are not at fault 
if they break their fast during the Month of Ramadan because they are unable to fast. 
They must each give a mudd (a specific measure) of food as charity for every day they 
break their fast, and they must make up each day they missed after Month of 
Ramadan.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 117 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 239 
• Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 297 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 215 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 42, H. 1950
reference.chapter: WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE UNABLE TO FAST; AN ELDERLY PERSON, A YOUNG PERSON,
reference.location: Volume 2 › WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE UNABLE TO FAST; AN ELDERLY PERSON, A YOUNG PERSON,
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٢، ح ١٩٥٠

hadiths.chapter: 42. WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE UNABLE TO FAST; AN ELDERLY PERSON, A YOUNG PERSON,
hadiths.en:
Abd al-Malik ibn Utbah al -Hashimi asked Abu al -Hasan {a.s} about an elderly 
man and an elderly woman who are too weak to fast during the Month of Ramadan.  
Imam {a.s} said: "They should give a mudd (a measure) of wheat as charity for each day."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 42, H. 1951
reference.chapter: WHAT HAS BEEN SAID ABOUT THOSE WHO ARE UNABLE TO FAST; AN ELDERLY PERSON, A YOUNG PERSON,

hadiths.chapter: 43. CHAPTER ON REWARDS OF ONE WHO PROVIDES
hadiths.ar:
1953  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم « 
هَّلْ ي َّذْرِى  إ َّى  ُّ لَّي َّالٍ هَّذ ِةِ مَّا ذ َّإك َّ ق َّق َّالَّ لَّهُّ إ َّن ِى 
إ َّن ْ ي ُّعْي ِق َّ كُّل َّ لَّي ْلَّه ٍ مِن ْ هَّذ ِةِ إَّلل َّي َّالِى  عَّس ْزَّ رِق َّاب ٍ مِن ْ وُّلْذِ إ ِسْمَّاعِي ل إ َّن ْث َّ وَّ إ ُّمِّى  لأَّ ي َّن ْلُّغ ُّ مَّالِى   
ذ َّإك َّ ق َّمَّا ر َّإلَّ ي َّن ْق ُّصُّ جَّي  َّى ن َّلَّغ َّ ن ِهِ  رَّق َّن َّه ً وَّإجِذَّة ً ق ِى  كُّلِّ ذ َّلِك َّ ي َّق ُّولُّ لأَّ إ َّق ْذِرُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق َّق َّالَّ لَّه 
كُّلِّ لَّي ْلَّه ٍ رَّج ُّلاً مُّسْلِماً رَّذْب ُّ ي َّا سَّ ذِي زُّ إ ِن  َّ إ ِق ْطَّارَّك َّ  
إ َّج َّاك َّ إَّلْمُّسْلِمَّ ي َّعْذِلُّ عِي ْق َّ رَّق َّن َّه ٍ مِن ْ وُّلْذِ إ ِسْمَّاعِي لَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم
hadiths.en:
Abu al-Sabbah al-Kinani narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"Whoever provides iftar to a  fasting person will have a reward equal to that of the 
fasting person."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 137 • Iqbal Al-Aamal, Part 
1, p. 7 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 43, H. 1952
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REWARDS OF ONE WHO PROVIDES
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON REWARDS OF ONE WHO PROVIDES
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٣، ح ١٩٥٢

hadiths.chapter: 43. CHAPTER ON REWARDS OF ONE WHO PROVIDES
hadiths.ar: 1954   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّوسَّى ن ْن ُّ ن َّكْزٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال لُّ مِن ْ صِي َّامِك
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Sadir entered upon my father {a.s} during the Month of Ramadan.  
My father said to him: 'O Sadir, do you know what nights these are?'  
Sadir replied: 'Yes, may I be your ransom; these are the nights of the Month of 
Ramadan. Why do you ask?'  
My father {a.s} said to him: 'Can you free ten slaves from the descendants of Isma'il every 
night of these nights?'  
Sadir replied: 'May I be your ransom, my wealth cannot reach that amount.'   
My father {a.s} continued to reduce the number until he said: 'Can you free one slave 
every night?' Sadir replied: 'I cannot do that either.'  
My father {a.s} then asked: 'Can you provide iftar for one Muslim every night?'  
Sadir replied: 'Yes, I can, and even for ten Muslims.'  
My father {a.s} said: 'That is what I intended, O Sadir. Providing iftar to your Muslim 
brother is equal to freeing one slave from the descendants of Isma'il {a.s}.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 68 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 201 • 
Iqbal Al-Aamal, Part 1, p. 7 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 252 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 139 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 43, H. 1953
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REWARDS OF ONE WHO PROVIDES
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON REWARDS OF ONE WHO PROVIDES
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٣، ح ١٩٥٣

hadiths.chapter: 43. CHAPTER ON REWARDS OF ONE WHO PROVIDES
hadiths.ar:
1955   -  وَّ كَّان َّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : إ ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ إَّلْي َّوْمُّ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي َّصُّومُّ ق ِي هِ إ َّمَّزَّ ن ِس َّاة ٍ ق َّي ذ ْن َّحُّ وَّ ي ُّق ْطَّغُّ إ َّعْص َّاو ُّةُّ 
وَّ ي ُّطْن َّح ُّ ق َّا ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ عِن ْذَّ إَّلْمَّسَّاءِ إ َّكَّث  َّ عَّلَّى إَّلْق ُّذُّورِ جَّي  َّى ي َّح ِذَّ رِي حَّ إَّلْمَّ زَّق ِ وَّ هُّوَّ صَّان ِم  ي ُّم َّ ي َّق ُّول 
إِع ْزِق ُّوإ لأ ِلِ ق ُّلاَّن ٍ إِع ْزِق ُّوإ لأ ِلِ ق ُّلاَّن ٍ
hadiths.en:
Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Abu al-Hasan {a.s} that he said:  
"Providing iftar to your fasting brother is more virtuous than your own fasting."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 396 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 68 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 201 • Misbah Al-Mutahajjud, Vol. 2, p. 626 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 137 
• Iqbal Al-Aamal, Part 1, p. 7 • Al-Misbah (by Al-Kafami) Vol. 1, p. 632 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 251 • Wasa’il Al-
Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 139 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 43, H. 1954
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REWARDS OF ONE WHO PROVIDES
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON REWARDS OF ONE WHO PROVIDES
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٣، ح ١٩٥٤

hadiths.chapter: 43. CHAPTER ON REWARDS OF ONE WHO PROVIDES
hadiths.ar:
1956  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ لِك َّ عِن ْذَّ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ 
ج َّل َّ عِي ْق ُّ رَّق َّن َّه ٍ وَّ مَّع ْق ِزَّة   لِمَّا مَّص َّى مِن ْ ذ ُّن ُّون ِهِ   لَّي ْسَّ كُّل ُّن َّا ن َّق ْذِرُّ عَّلَّى إ َّن ْ ن ُّق َّطِّزَّ صَّان ِماً ق َّق َّالَّ  
« لَّن َّن ٍ ي ُّق َّطِّزُّ ن ِهَّا صَّان ِماً إ َّوْ س َّزْن َّه ٍ 
مِن ْ مَّاءِ عَّذ ْب ٍ إ َّ وْ ي ُّمَّي ْزَّإب ٍ لأَّ ي َّق ْذِرُّ عَّلَّى إ َّكْي َّزَّ مِن ْ ذ َّلِك َّ.
hadiths.en:
Imam Zain al-Abidin Ali ibn al-Hussein {a.s} would, on the day he fasted, order 
a sheep to be slaughtered, its parts to be cut, and then cooked. When it was evening, 
he would lean over the pots to smell the aroma of the broth while still fasting.  
Then Imam {a.s} would say: "Bring the dishes and serve portions to the family of so-and-
so, and to the family of so-and-so."  
Afterward, bread and dates would be brought to Imam {a.s}, and that would be his 
dinner.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 396 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 68 • Al-
Manaqib, Part 4, p. 155 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 224 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 140 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 
46, p. 71 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 93, p. 317 • A’lam al-Uloom, Vol. 18, p. 132 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 43, H. 1955

hadiths.chapter: 43. CHAPTER ON REWARDS OF ONE WHO PROVIDES
hadiths.en:
The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said:   
"Whoever provides iftar to a believing fasting person during this month will have, for 
that act, the reward of freeing a slave and forgiveness for their past sins from Allah {SWT}, 
the Exalted and Glorious."  
It was said to Him {saws}: "O Messenger of Allah {SWT}, not all of us are able to provide iftar 
to a fasting person."  
He (peace be upon him and his family) replied:  "Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Blessed and 
Exalted, is generous. He grants this reward to those among you who can only offer a 
sip of milk to break the fast of a fasting person, or a drink of pure water, or a few dates 
if they are unable to offer more than that."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Makarim al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 137 • Iqbal Al-Aamal, Part 
1, p. 7 • Al-Misbah (by Al-Kafami) Vol. 1, p. 632 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 252

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 43, H. 1956

hadiths.chapter: 44. CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR
hadiths.ar:
1958  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ سَّمَّاعَّه ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلس َّحُّورِ لِمَّن ْ إ َّرَّإذَّ إَّلص َّوْمَّ ق َّق َّال ى  س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان َّ ق َّا ِن  َّ 
إَّلْق َّص ْلَّ ق ِى  إَّلس َّحُّورِ وَّ لَّوْ ن ِس َّزْن َّه ٍ مِن ْ مَّاءٍ وَّ إ َّم َّا ق ِى  إَّلي  َّطَّو ُّعِ ق َّمَّن ْ إ َّجَّث  َّ  إ َّن ْ ي َّي َّسَّح َّزَّ ق َّلْي َّق ْعَّلْ وَّ مَّن ْ لَّمْ ي َّق ْعَّلْ ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ.
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his family) said:  
"Suhoor (the pre-dawn meal) is a blessing."  
He {saws} also said:  "My Ummah should not abandon suhoor, even if it is just a single 
piece of dried date."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 44, H. 1957

hadiths.chapter: 44. CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR
hadiths.ar:
1959  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ إ َّن ُّو ن َّصِي زٍ : عَّن ِ إَّلس َّحُّورِ لِمَّن ْ إ َّرَّإذَّ إَّلص َّوْمَّ إ َّ وَّإج ِث   هُّوَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ق َّق َّال  ي َّي َّسَّح َّزَّ إ ِن ْ س َّاءَّ 
ق َّا َّم َّا ق ِى  س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان َّ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ إ َّق ْص َّلُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّي َّسَّح َّزَّ إ ُّجِث  ُّ إ َّن ْ لأَّ ي َّي ْزُّك َّ ق ِى  س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان
hadiths.en:
Sama'ah asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about suhoor for someone intending to fast.  
Imam {a.s} said: "As for the Month of Ramadan, it is recommended to have suhoor, even 
if it is just a sip of water. As for voluntary fasting, whoever wishes to have suhoor may 
do so, and whoever does not, there is no harm."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 94 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 197 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 314 • Misbah Al-Mutahajjud, Vol. 2, p. 626 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 241 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 143 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 44, H. 1958
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٤، ح ١٩٥٨

hadiths.chapter: 44. CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR
hadiths.ar: 1960  - وَّ ق َّالَّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ ض لَّى ق ِي َّامِ إَّلل َّي ْلِ
hadiths.en:
Abu Basir asked Imam {a.s} about suhoor for someone intending to fast, saying, 
"Is it obligatory?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "There is no harm if one does not have suhoor if they wish. However, 
in the Month of Ramadan, it is better to have suhoor. I prefer that it not be abandoned 
in the Month of Ramadan."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 94 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 241 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 142 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 44, H. 1959

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR

hadiths.chapter: 44. CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR
hadiths.ar:
1961   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ َّمِي زِ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلن  َّن ِى ِّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال   «
وَّ مَّلاَّن ِكَّي َّهُّ ي ُّصَّل ُّون َّ عَّلَّى إَّلْمُّسْي َّع ْق ِزِي ن َّ وَّ إَّلْمُّي َّسَّحِّزِي ن َّ ن ِالْأ َّسْحَّارِ  ق َّلْي َّي َّسَّح َّزْ إ َّجَّذُّكُّمْ وَّ لَّوْ ن ِس َّزْن َّه ٍ مِن ْ مَّاءٍ.    
وإ ق ص ل إلسحور إلسوي ق  وإلي مز ، ومطلق  لك  إلطعام وإلس زإب  إ لى إ ن  ي سي ي ق ن  طلوع إلق ح ز.
hadiths.en:
The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said:   
"Strengthen yourselves for the fasting of the day by eating suhoor, and for the night 
prayer by taking a nap during the midday rest (qaylulah)."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Muqni’ah, Part 1, p. 316 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, 
p. 199 • Rawdat Al-Wa'azizin, Vol. 2, p. 341 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 243 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 144 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 44, H. 1960
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR

hadiths.chapter: 44. CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR
hadiths.ar: 1962  - وَّ سَّا َّلَّ رَّج ُّل  إَّلص َّاذِق َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّالَّ: إ كُّلُّ وَّ إ َّن َّا إ َّس ُّك  ُّ ق ِى  إَّلْق َّح ْزِ ق َّق َّال ك
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} that 
the Prophet {saws} (peace be upon him and his family) said:  
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Blessed and Exalted, and His angels send blessings upon those 
who seek forgiveness and those who partake in suhoor during the pre-dawn hours. So 
let one of you partake in suhoor, even if it is just a sip of water."   
[AL SADUQ] 
The best suhoor is a mixture of barley flour (sawiq) and dates, and you are permitted 
to eat and drink until you are certain that dawn has risen.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 243 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 44, H. 1961
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٤، ح ١٩٦١

hadiths.chapter: 44. CHAPTER ON THE REWARD OF SUHOOR
hadiths.ar: 1963   -  وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ى إ َّن ْ ي َّصُّومُّوإ إَّلذ َّهْز
hadiths.en:
A man asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, "I eat while I am unsure 
if dawn has risen."  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Eat until you are no longer in doubt."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 120 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 44, H. 1962
